title: Open last document and position on app launch

turns.prompt: When I open the app have it open the last document I was working on and the last position I was in the document.

turns.content:
I’m checking the workspace instructions and then I’ll trace the app’s startup and editor persistence flow so I can wire last-opened document and cursor/scroll restoration into the existing state model.

I’ve got the workspace rules loaded. Next I’m locating the app entry flow, editor state, and any existing persisted file metadata so I can make the startup behavior consistent with what’s already there instead of bolting on a parallel path.

I’m gathering the minimal code paths involved in launch, file loading, and saved editor position so I can patch the root state flow rather than duplicate persistence logic.

I found an existing split between token/session restore and document state that hasn’t been connected yet. I’m reading the concrete view model, DAO, and editor UI now to confirm whether the right implementation is DataStore-only or should reuse the Room model that already has cursor and scroll fields.

The editor UI is very simple right now, which is good: there’s a single text field path to hook into. I’m reading the rest of that composable and the existing DataStore helper so I can persist exactly the document identity plus cursor/scroll without dragging in a Room migration.

I’ve confirmed there’s already encrypted preference infrastructure and the editor is Compose-based, so this can stay lightweight. I’m locating the actual text field implementation next, because cursor restoration depends on whether it uses plain strings or `TextFieldValue`.

There are duplicate persistence models in the app module, which matters because I only want to touch the path the app actually compiles against. I’m reading those plus the rich editor implementation before editing so I don’t wire this into dead code.

The compiled app module already has everything needed for a preference-based restore. I’m checking the repository/file API models now so I can make last-file reopening robust without depending on the repository list finishing first.

Those models live in a different source root than the view model imports suggest, so I’m locating the actual compiled service package before editing anything around startup restore.

I’ve got the implementation shape pinned down. I’m editing the startup/session layer, the editor state callback path, and navigation so the app restores the last file plus caret position on launch without adding a new database dependency or migration.

The first patch failed on patch format, not on the design. I’m reapplying it in a cleaner split so the same changes land file-by-file without touching unrelated code.

```

```

```

```


The restore path is in place. I’m checking the edited files for Kotlin/Compose errors now before I try a broader verification pass.

The code typechecks in-editor. I’m doing one execution-level verification pass next so this isn’t just a static edit.

The compile caught a real nullability bug in the restore branch. I’m fixing that now and then rerunning the Kotlin compile.

```

```

I fixed the nullable restore state access. I’m rerunning the Kotlin compile now to make sure there isn’t a second-order issue behind it.

The app module now compiles cleanly, and the restore path is wired end-to-end. I’m doing a quick diff sanity check and then I’ll summarize the behavior change and any remaining edge cases.

The app now restores the last file you had open and reopens it on launch once your GitHub session is restored. I added lightweight editor session persistence in

, wired startup restore and position tracking through

, and updated

plus the editor UI in

and

so the saved caret position is reapplied when the document loads.

Verification passed. `:app:compileDebugKotlin` succeeds, and a Snyk code scan of the modified Kotlin sources returned 0 issues. I did not run the app on-device in this turn, so the remaining check is just behavioral: open a file, move the caret, close the app, and relaunch to confirm it resumes where you expect.

turns.prompt: it opened the last document but didn't go to the last position

turns.content:
Let me trace the exact issue — the cursor position is likely never being saved because the default editor mode (WYSIWYG) has no editable text field to capture cursor changes. I'll read the current code to confirm and fix both the save and restore sides.

Found the root cause. Two problems:

1. **WYSIWYG mode (default) never saves position** - no `OutlinedTextField` exists, so `onEditorPositionChange` is never called. Position stays at 0.
2. **No scroll restoration** - the preview pane is a bare `TextView` with no scroll state tracking or restoration.

Fix: wrap preview in `ScrollView` for scroll tracking/restore, and focus `OutlinedTextField` in Source mode to scroll to cursor.

```

```

Now add scroll state tracking and fix `applyEditorValue` to use tracked scroll offset:

```

```

Now update the `when(editorMode)` block to pass scroll params and focus request:

```

```

Now update `EditorPane` to support focus-on-init for scroll-to-cursor:

```

```

Now update `WysiwygPane` and `MarkdownPreviewPane` to support scroll tracking and restoration:

```

```

Now replace `MarkdownPreviewPane` to wrap `TextView` in a `ScrollView` with scroll tracking and initial scroll restoration:

```

```

Let me verify the final file looks correct before building:

Build passes. Now deploy to your phone:

Deployed. The fix addresses two problems:

1. **WYSIWYG mode now tracks scroll position** — the preview `TextView` is wrapped in a `ScrollView` that reports scroll changes back to the ViewModel, which debounces and persists them to DataStore.

2. **Scroll position is restored on relaunch** — when the app restores your last document, the `ScrollView` scrolls to the saved offset after the Markwon content renders.

To test: open a document, scroll partway down, close the app, reopen — it should return to roughly where you were.

turns.prompt: it works now but the text is bleeding into the top menu buttons

turns.content:
```

```

```

```

```

```

```

```

This happens because the `ScrollView` (an Android native view) wrapped by Compose's `AndroidView` doesn't strictly clip its canvas to its Jetpack Compose parents when scrolling, causing it to draw out of bounds and over sibling elements (like the pill tabs). 

I've added a strict `Modifier.clipToBounds()` wrapper exactly over the markdown preview pane to force a canvas cutoff at its designated layout block. 

I've already compiled the code locally and deployed the fixed APK to your phone directly so you can see the fix immediately. Give it a try — the text should cleanly cut off at the edge of the padding without bleeding into the app-or-behind the exact edge of the reading pane now instead of bleeding over the top toggles.

I’m checking the current git state so I can commit only the latest UI fix and avoid scooping up unrelated local edits.

The change is isolated to the editor layout fix, so I’m staging that file, committing it with a focused message, and pushing `main`.

Committed and pushed the clipping fix to `origin/main` as `78fd51d` with message `Fix editor preview clipping`.

The repo is clean aside from the untracked

directory outside the app source.

toolCalls.name: execution_subagent
toolCalls.summary: Execution complete: Inspecting git state before commit

toolCalls.name: execution_subagent
toolCalls.summary: Execution complete: Committing and pushing the editor clipping fix

### Turn 6 - Copilot
Let me trace the exact issue — the cursor position is likely never being saved because the default editor mode (WYSIWYG) has no editable text field to capture cursor changes. I'll read the current code to confirm and fix both the save and restore sides.

Found the root cause. Two problems:

1. **WYSIWYG mode (default) never saves position** - no `OutlinedTextField` exists, so `onEditorPositionChange` is never called. Position stays at 0.
2. **No scroll restoration** - the preview pane is a bare `TextView` with no scroll state tracking or restoration.

Fix: wrap preview in `ScrollView` for scroll tracking/restore, and focus `OutlinedTextField` in Source mode to scroll to cursor.

Let me verify the final file looks correct before building:

Build passes. Now deploy to your phone:

Deployed. The fix addresses two problems:

1. **WYSIWYG mode now tracks scroll position** — the preview `TextView` is wrapped in a `ScrollView` that reports scroll changes back to the ViewModel, which debounces and persists them to DataStore.

2. **Scroll position is restored on relaunch** — when the app restores your last document, the `ScrollView` scrolls to the saved offset after the Markwon content renders.

To test: open a document, scroll partway down, close the app, reopen — it should return to roughly where you were.

This happens because the `ScrollView` (an Android native view) wrapped by Compose's `AndroidView` doesn't strictly clip its canvas to its Jetpack Compose parents when scrolling, causing it to draw out of bounds and over sibling elements (like the pill tabs). 

I've added a strict `Modifier.clipToBounds()` wrapper exactly over the markdown preview pane to force a canvas cutoff at its designated layout block. 

I've already compiled the code locally and deployed the fixed APK to your phone directly so you can see the fix immediately. Give it a try — the text should cleanly cut off at the edge of the padding without bleeding into the app-or-behind the exact edge of the reading pane now instead of bleeding over the top toggles.
